summary: The diff implements all four requirements with substantial coverage. REQ-1 (Footer redesign), REQ-2 (Roboto font), REQ-3 (HeroSettingsEditor expansion), and REQ-4 (particle effects) are all addressed. A few specific acceptance criteria have gaps worth noting, particularly around animated preview thumbnails for particle presets and the hero height selector options.

missing_requirements.requirement: Each particle preset should display a small animated preview thumbnail in the admin gallery/grid.
missing_requirements.reason: The HeroSettingsEditor shows particle presets using emoji icons and descriptions but there is no evidence of actual animated canvas-based or CSS-animated preview thumbnails for each preset in the grid. The diff shows emoji plus text labels, not animated mini-canvases.

missing_requirements.requirement: Hero height selector should support options: compact, normal, and full-screen.
missing_requirements.reason: The IdleHeroSettings type in both backend/main.mo and backend/migration.mo defines heroHeight as only a single variant normal. The compact and full-screen variants are not present. The HeroSettingsEditor and Home.tsx do not appear to apply different height classes based on this setting.

hallucinated_features.description: A Network particle preset (11th preset) was added beyond the 10 required presets.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies at minimum 10 named presets. The diff adds an 11th preset Network with connected nodes description. This was not in the requested preset list.

hallucinated_features.description: A backend migration module was added as a separate file backend/migration.mo.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request constraints state all Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file. Adding a separate migration.mo module may violate this constraint.

hallucinated_features.description: caffeine.ai attribution was added to the Footer.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any third-party attribution in the footer. The frontend-file-summaries.txt mentions caffeine.ai attribution in the Footer description, which was not requested.
